title: frangula azorica
intro: Frangula azorica, also known as Madeira buckthorn, is a plant species native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including being a natural laxative due to its high concentration of anthraquinones. The health benefits of Frangula azorica include treating constipation, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and blood pressure. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
